---
title: "Module 05: Advanced Models"
---

## Learning objectives

- Name **random forests**, **gradient boosting**, and **small neural nets** as standard tabular learners after a single tree.
- **Swap `parsnip` specs** inside the Tuesday **`workflow()`** — same recipe and folds.
- Know which **Day 4** blocks demo PCA, imputation, imbalance, and VIP.

[← Module library](index.qmd) · [Module 04 — Pipeline](module-04-pipeline.qmd) · [Module 08 — Compare learners](module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd)

---

## On the slides (Day 4)

| Part | Topic | Slide link |
|------|--------|------------|
| A | Recap `rec_base`, stressed scenarios | [Day 4 deck](../slides/day-04-thursday.html) |
| B | RF, XGB, MLP, model catalog, **accuracy** compare | [Part B — Models](../slides/day-04-thursday.html#/part-b-models) |
| C | `step_pca()` | [Part C — PCA](../slides/day-04-thursday.html#/part-c-pca) |
| D | `step_impute_*()` | [Part D — Impute](../slides/day-04-thursday.html#/part-d-impute) |
| E | Imbalance + metrics | [Part E — Metrics](../slides/day-04-thursday.html#/part-e-metrics) |
| F | VIP | [Part F — VIP](../slides/day-04-thursday.html#/part-f-vip) |

Lecture uses **`metrics_acc <- metric_set(accuracy)`** in Parts B–D; full metric set in Part E.

---

## Key message

**Same preprocessing + cross-validation + score sheet**, different **fitting engine**. More flexibility demands **stricter** resampling — not skipping the “tiny \(n\) penguins” conversation.

---

## Parsnip: type → engine → mode

| Model type | Valid engines (examples) | Mode |
|------------|--------------------------|------|
| `decision_tree()` | `rpart`, `C5.0` | classification, regression |
| `rand_forest()` | `ranger`, `randomForest` | classification, regression |
| `boost_tree()` | `xgboost`, `lightgbm` | classification, regression |
| `logistic_reg()` | `glm`, `glmnet` | classification |
| `mlp()` | `nnet`, `keras` | classification, regression |

Discover knobs: `extract_parameter_set_dials(spec)` · [tidymodels model list](https://www.tidymodels.org/find/parsnip/).

---

## Model families

### Random forests (bagging)

Many trees on **bootstrap** samples; each split sees a **random subset** of predictors → lower variance than one tree.

Example intuition: with 5 predictors, a forest might sample 2 at each split. One tree may overreact to a noisy bill measurement; averaging 400 trees makes that noise average out.

### Boosting (XGBoost)

Trees added **sequentially** to correct previous errors. [Boosting boundary slides](../slides/day-04-thursday.html#/part-b-models) show probability surfaces sharpening over rounds.

Toy narrative: round 1 misses several borderline Gentoo points, round 2 focuses extra split capacity on those residual errors, and later rounds refine that boundary.

### Neural networks (tabular)

`mlp()` with `nnet` — flexible but data-hungry; compare honestly in [Module 08](module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd).

On small tabular \(n\), gains can be unstable because nets depend heavily on tuning, scaling, and feature representation; trees often match or beat them with less tuning effort.

---

## Recipe extensions (same workflow)

| Step | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `step_pca()` | Compress correlated morphometrics / OTUs inside CV |
| `step_impute_*()` | Fill missing values without dropping rows before split |
| `step_upsample()` / `step_downsample()` | Training-time class balance — not always helpful if classes are already separable |

Details: [Module 06 — Metrics](module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd).

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
rec_ext <- recipe(y ~ ., data = train) |>
  step_impute_median(all_numeric_predictors()) |>
  step_impute_mode(all_nominal_predictors()) |>
  step_pca(all_numeric_predictors(), num_comp = 10)

wf_ext <- workflow() |>
  add_recipe(rec_ext) |>
  add_model(boost_tree() |> set_engine("xgboost") |> set_mode("classification"))
```

---

## Course links

- **Slides:** [Day 4 (Thursday)](../slides/day-04-thursday.html)
- **Pipeline:** [Module 04](module-04-pipeline.qmd)
- **Compare engines:** [Module 08](module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd)
- **Metrics & SHAP:** [Module 06](module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd)
- **Notebook:** [penguins-classification.Rmd](../notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd)
